transcript.text: Hello.
Hello.
How's it going?
meeting is being recorded.
I'm great.
How are you all doing?
Good.
Thank you.
Thank you.
It's going to be just the two of us because Atul is on a break.
Okay.
Well, I don't think we got a chance to meet yet officially.
So I'm Bailey.
I'm sort of the engagement manager who's going to be handling your account with GrowthX.
And I know there's been a little bit of some changing hands over the last month or so.
Yeah.
So, you know, if you have any concerns at all, let me know.
I'm sort of here to hopefully provide a stable managing experience for you all.
Thank you.
Nice to meet you.
you.
So I will share our agenda for today.
So,
So I wanted to say sort of the big headline here is that we have a lot of blog posts in review with your team right now.
So if we want to continue kind of publishing through the holidays, we'll need you all to take a look at those and turn some of those around.
Because I think we have, in our publishing one-a-day cadence, I think we have four lined up.
So that would take us to, you know, Tuesday.
But beyond that, we don't have anything reviewed or ready to go.
So the list of the ones that are to be reviewed are, I see eight plus the competitive.
Let's see.
Yes, here, I can just share my, why don't I share.
Yeah.
That screen instead of.
So we have, the ones we have.
In review, we have a few that aren't assigned a reviewer yet, and so it may be that, is this something that you wanted us to assign to you, or were you assigning, self-assigning these?
Okay, so I think we need to differentiate between the ones above that and the competitive ones.
Okay.
The competitive ones are more challenging.
I think they, they won't be ready before, so we are, we're going, the company here goes on a break between the 24th to the 31st.
Okay.
Or second, I would say, sorry.
I, I don't expect those four to be ready in the next three days, four days, whatever, you know, because I think they, they, need to discuss the structure and the...

There's some foundational, there's some fundamental issues with the blog itself, because they replicate one that we did that was very good with zero eyes, but they replicate it in a way that doesn't make sense for the other competitors.
Those are, we need to recalibrate, and we can talk about that, but I don't expect it, I don't expect us to be able to turn them around even if we want to.
Plus, because they're competitive, I want additional reviews from our SEs, and so that will just take more time.
Yeah, I think that's fine, and actually we're still waiting on them.
I know you mentioned you might have some battle cards to share with us, which can really help kind of add that extra layer.
We have some battle cards, I think, I didn't realize that tool was off the entire week, so we'll...
Latest on Monday, you'll get that, but if we can share what we have.
We can share.
I think we don't have a ton.
Yeah, we have a couple.
But I think we need to talk about structure and kind of understand the individual competitors.
It's much of a copy-paste of a blog that we did for Zero Eyes, which is a very focused company on the gun detection use case.
And that's pretty much all they do.
And so it made a lot of sense to say us versus them on gun detection.
But the other vendors are more general purpose in terms of use case.
Now, we may want to have a chapter on gun detection within the blog because it's a very important use case.
And that's one where we may...
Have a significant differentiation on all of these, but it can't just be on gun detection.
It would be, that would mean that we would have to have multiple blog posts for the same vendor on other use cases too.
And I think, I don't know, we should discuss that.
Is that the idea?
Because it seems like.
Yeah.

So I think often the way we do this with this type of blog post is we'll either use your battle cards, or I also shared, if you don't have battle cards, we can sort of build a matrix that's either based around features or around use cases.

I think what we need is sort of the context on which use cases you want us to either feature or kind of leave out, and whether we should be talking about kind of the full landscape, and then kind of what product mentions we want to tie that back to, and then any kind of notes on positioning against specific competitors.
And this we can use to create.
Create essentially a competitive positioning guide artifact in the same way that we created your writing guidelines.
So when we create this specific comparison type of content, we can actually kind of pull specifically from that document.
So I think you can either share what you have and we can start to fill that out or we can have a separate meeting and kind of talk through this or your team can fill it out.
Whatever makes the most sense to get us context and then we'll revisit those articles.
Yeah, so we'll share what we have.
think one thing you could do on your end to start is just use one of the models to do a company profile of the competitor based on public available information.
And just, you know, that's what I would do to start with just to make sure that I capture the entirety of what has been published around them.
And then we can add our own.
And
Point of view, knowing what we know from the field.
So, but in some cases, we kind of need to do the online search anyway, because some of these are relatively small and early.
We may not have 100% visibility in exactly what they're doing.
And so getting a baseline from a, this is what the company does and what they're claiming and blah, blah, blah.
We can take it from there as a starting point.
Okay, that makes sense.
I'll talk to Mariana and see, maybe send us what you have, and then we can kind of figure out what gaps we need to fill in, and we can kind of make a plan on how to approach this content from there.
And then we'll talk about structure for each one of them, because they're all a little different, but they're definitely different than zeroes.
Yeah, I think that makes sense.
And I think having that information going in, in terms of what use cases or features to focus on, will help us determine the structure.
Yeah.
Yeah.
So, then in terms of other reviews, do you feel the others are just one ask to just do it.
Like, we just discussed it coming into the meeting, we know.
We'll do our best to get you the revisions.
Okay.
That sounds great.
Now, are there some that are… So, let's just review the table again.
One second.
So, you have… You have a bunch of… From the top, you have a bunch of client-approved ideas, right?
Yes.
We've got a good runway of articles.
Good.
So, you've got enough top of the funnel, for lack of a better term.
Yeah.
Then, there's… 11 to 18 are the ones that I'd like to kind of get you feedback.
Exactly, because then we could sort of have a publishing runway through the holidays while you're off.
And then revision needed is for you, right?
Yes, so Mariana is working on those and turning those around.
Okay, and then you have four that are ready to publish.
So assuming the revision needed, you know, will be enough?
You may have six.
Yes, like hopefully, she'll should be turning those around, I believe, today.
So assuming that those are good to go, then yeah, you can consider we have six.
have six, and then we have that list.
Yeah, so we'll get on them.
So yeah, I think even if you can just give us a few of those to make sure we're publishing a few, you know, on that, that we're not totally off until the 5th of January, think that'll be great.
Okay, good.
I think you, I mean, I...
I feel good about being able to review some of these that are ready to review, also, you know, send more of the ideas, right, if you can.
Yeah, so Marianna's working on five more, so we'll also be sending you those to review today or tomorrow as well.
Okay.
Once, you know, Monday, Tuesday will be pretty light for me, so I'm just probably reviewing the blog.
Yeah.
Great.
And I know it's a tricky time of year, so just trying to make sure we have a few kind of queued up to publish so that we keep that regular schedule is great.
Yeah, but I like the one-a-day rhythm.
Yeah.
And we need to try to keep it.
Okay.
So with that, we may, if you, I think I wanted to touch base with you on setting up auto-publishing in Webflow, which essentially means that Marianna could publish.
which is just trying to keep up
Great from our system when you've approved a post, instead of having to go through our kind of publishing ops person who puts them on manually.
And I think this is great.

This is a great way to kind of increase velocity because it just means that as soon as you say that a post is good to go, she can just hit play on it instead of having to then submit a ticket and wait for someone else to do it manually.

So I think I believe this is something we can do with the Webflow access you gave us, but I just wanted to check and make sure this is something you would be kind of comfortable with before you did it.
Yeah, yeah.
Okay, great.
Okay.
And then in terms of holiday, I know you're offline, but I plan to send an async update tomorrow just to give you a sense of what we're working on, when you can expect it, what will be published.
So you sort of have a source to refer back to in case you have any questions and also let you know.
Because some of us will be on vacation, kind of who your point of contact is if I am on vacation and something comes up.
Yeah.
Okay.
Okay.
So do you want to take a minute to chat about images?
Because I know you wanted to revisit that image generation pipeline.
Did I not add Nicole?
I think she's here.
Oh, okay.
Good.
I want her to...
here.
Hi.
Hi, Nicole.
Hi.
Stealth mode.
Okay.
But yeah, let's talk about images.

So I pulled together some kind of different examples because I wasn't sure exactly what you were thinking because we do all sorts of different types of images, more like stock imagery, more kind of just, you know, graphic imagery.
And then we also is something we've been doing for other clients that sometimes within the blog post itself.
Kind of creating these basic infographics based on the content of the blog post itself.
So these are just kind of like a couple examples of what that might look like.
So if that's something you'd be interested in, we could also add that as a separate step in the pipeline.
So this would be in addition to kind of the header image if you felt like you wanted something to break up the text itself.
That's something that we've been doing that's been performing pretty well.
I definitely think infographics are a huge help, but we are in the midst of rebranding.
We're at like the early stages.
So we need to develop a style before most things get changed.
That's fair.
So then, yeah, I guess my question for you, given wherever you are in kind of your rebrand or like your design updates, what is...
Is it at this stage that you would sort of want to see from those header images that's different than what you're seeing now?
I definitely think we should lean more stock photos.
I don't think the just kind of text images work well because it's, you know, like duplicating the title.
Yeah.
Alberto, Mal, comment a lot.
I think that by general, it's going to be, depends.
I don't like what we currently have because I find it extremely repetitive and essentially you can't really see the background.
I think cases, stock images for sure.
I don't know what else to do, frankly, to do it in an automated fashion.
So I don't, we don't like the classic AI generated images.
People don't.
don't.
It well here if you do the classic nano banana stuff, but, you know, I think with visuals, you always have to react a little bit to the type, it's difficult.
I mean, I think it looks like, you know, we're using kind of a stock image base right now for your blog post.
So, I mean, I guess my question then would be sort of how are you feeling about the stock image component of it?
Is it just a matter of lightening the filter and taking off the text because you don't want it to be so text heavy?
Or is there something different you'd want to see from those sort of stock style images themselves?
I don't know.
I think we need to do a little exploration.
Okay.
That would be one thing.
I have a slightly different opinion than Nicole on the infographics.
I think having some.
Awesome.
At least one graphic in the text makes the blog a little less, you know, monotonous.
Having said that, you know, they're not necessarily easy to create, but I can also see some reuse of these infographic as social media posts here and there.
So there's some additional benefits there, but I think we should start from the header.
So then in terms of the image that you want to see in the header, so we'll continue sort of taking a stock image, using stock imagery, no text.
What types of things have you done?
Okay, so stock images, one.
What else have you been doing for others?
Yeah, I mean, here, let me, I can just pull up some of these examples.
see.
So I think we've done kind of a wide mix.
I mean, some companies really tend towards stock images, and sometimes that's more of like a realistic looking, you know, computer generated image.
Sometimes it's straight stock image.
Some, you know, are more people focused, like we have some where it's more people, it's more kind of, you know, situational.
We have some where it's really just more kind of graphics using a mix of the brand colors.
So sometimes it's more like abstract shapes and visuals, and sometimes it's more like icons.
Yeah.
You know, you know, for this is like a travel agent, AI travel agent, so more, you know, graphic imagery, but all kind of actual objects and all kind of like in the travel space.
Again, in the blue brand colors with like a pop of color because they wanted a less monochromatic site.
And then this is more kind of icon, icon based and some like product imagery, again, kind of just using those brand colors to tie together different types of posts.
So I think, I mean, ultimately, it's the image pipeline can kind of, I mean, I don't want to say it can produce anything, but there's a huge range of what it can use given the right inputs.
So I think we just need a better understanding of what it is you want to see.
And from there, we can come up with some options, which just needs a bit of guidance in terms of what you would.
Like to see there.
Yeah, I think we need to identify what we want to be.
Yeah, maybe this is part of your exploration, Nicole, to figure out how we make these, what's our approach to this.
Yeah, I've been looking into what other companies have been doing.
There's no question.
For now, it would be nice to create a library of our, perhaps our UI stuff and all that stuff.
I don't think we have it.
So for now, like stock images, like security-related imagery, it's probably, like...
I mean, stock images, in our case, are mostly going to be people in some setting.
I mean, to start for now, I can just have them read the text.
Like, if you don't look like it.
camera.
Yeah.
Yeah.
Yeah, that could be nice.
I don't know, but it's difficult to find, well, outside of our product, which I don't know how you feed into the pipeline, but the security camera angle for photography is kind of difficult to find.
I don't know how much, you know what I mean?
Like, our point of view is always a security camera, which is different than a regular photo that you take care of a person with an average camera, you know?
So if you want connection to our space, it should be a security camera when you do a point of view.
Anyway, yeah.
So my recommendation for now might just be to, I can tell, I mean, our designer has the,

Context since she did the first round and just say you'd like to see some options using a similar kind of approach with stock imagery, but without the text on it and see what she comes up with for kind of an incremental update.
And then it sounds like you're doing some exploration on brand and design on your end.
And then once you have kind of some more direction there, we can always do another update at that point.
Yeah.
I mean, I'm reflecting on discussions that have happened on other things, particularly the website.
I mean, if you, there's definitely a preference for not, more of the in-product or more of real shots versus, you know, the stylized designs.
Mm-hmm.
Abstract stuff based on what, you know, Shikhar, our CEO, and our CTO kind of told us when we were rethinking the website.
Having said that, there's also a functional aspect of this particular implementation.
So, yeah, I don't have an answer, but I know that they don't like the AI-generated graphics, the cartoonish type stuff.
Yeah.
That has been a recurring theme.
Doesn't necessarily make our life easier, but...
No, but I understand.
You want it to feel, you know, on brand and like it matches your brand and your product as well.
I mean, from the ones that you showed us, I'll say, I don't...
I don't...
I think there's some interesting aspects about, like what Lovable is doing, for example, you can always determine that that's a Lovable image once you get used to the brand.
I don't particularly like the one from the, I think it's a medical company or pharmaceutical.
Interwell, yeah, I think that's the one.
A little bit too first person.
These ones.
Yeah.
Yeah.
That probably doesn't apply to us.
This one has some interesting aspects because there's some product, but they're a pure software company, which is different from us.
We have, this is for developers, so it makes a lot of sense.
Yeah.
I think the other one, not the travel agency, but the one before the travel agency forgot.
Sort of, this is another company that does.
does.
um.
I
Stock imagery as well, and sort of another, they do colors and then they'll add, you know, text in their brand colors, but it's sort of another example.
So this is interesting because in their stock image, they're adding text, but it's not the copy paste of the blog title.
Yeah.
Which I think would be a good starting point for us too.

Maybe in short term, the simplification would be, let's not just copy the same image, so the same text, and make the background of the image a little lighter so that you can actually see there's something, because in most cases you can't really see anything.
So I think those could be short-term adjustments while Nicole with things are approached to this.
Okay.
And is your preference in terms of like...
Keeping text on the image, but have it be something that speaks more to the value of the article versus just kind of a straight, you know, repurpose of the title.
Probably.
I'd also say shift around the text, obviously kind of depending on the different image, because it does, like, having basically the same image with the text doesn't look good.
Yeah.
Yeah, and I can see, and also, I mean, a lot of the titles are longer, too.
So if you're sort of using a shorter snippet of text, I think there's more opportunity to show the image itself.
Okay.
So I can bring that to our designer and just ask her to do a round of revisions, and then we should be able to share some more examples with you.
Yeah.
What do you think?
Good.
Great.
That's awesome.
I think I'll just double check.
Sorry.
Agenda, I think we covered, oh, the one other thing I wanted to ask you about is, I was wondering if you'd considered or if you had discussed adding FAQs to the end of the articles?
We're open to adding FAQs.
We talked about wanting to do a glossary.
would like to do a glossary.
Yeah, anything.

We've found that adding like standard sort of three to five question FAQ plus an FAQ schema on the back end of blog post templates, it's something that can give things a lift for SEO, but particularly for AEO, for LLMs, tend to like really pull from them.
So it's sort of something that we're starting to recommend to clients if it feels on brand for them and they're open to it across your content.
So that's something.
I can ask them to update your pipeline to start including that.
then I think because when we go in and set up the auto-publishing, we can also check and see if there's already a schema on the back end of your blog.
And if not, I think our engineering team can just add it when they do the auto-publishing.
Okay.
Okay.

And then Mariana had one thing she wanted me to share with you is that she's starting to build kind of a library of statistics and sources that, you know, are authoritative to pull into your blog post to make sure we're using kind of high-quality sources.

So if there are kind of particular organizations that you want us to prioritize including or to stay away from, it doesn't have to be live on this call, but just something to think about letting us know so we can make sure we're kind of pulling from the right sources for your content.
Yeah.
One that you can certainly, I can already tell you is
SIA.
Okay.
So I would look at that and then we'll think about it.
Yeah.
Okay.
I will share that with her.
Great.
I think those were, I think we've covered just about everything.
Was there anything else in the last minute or two that you all wanted to touch base on?
No, all good for now.
Okay.
Well, then thank you very much.
And I will send you all an async update so you kind of know what's happening while over the next couple weeks over the holidays.
Awesome.
Thank you.
All right.
Thanks, everyone.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Sync on blog content, image strategy, and process improvements.](https://fathom.video/share/as5MnWfH8BEhoZnsPXoXcLXFRr9TrzU2?tab=summary&timestamp=48.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Holiday Content Risk:** The 1-post/day cadence is at risk for the Dec 24–Jan 2 holiday break, as only 4 posts are ready. Everest will prioritize reviewing 11 other drafts to build a publishing buffer.](https://fathom.video/share/as5MnWfH8BEhoZnsPXoXcLXFRr9TrzU2?tab=summary&timestamp=529.0)
  - [**Competitive Content Blocked:** Four competitive analysis drafts are on hold. Their structure, copied from a Zero Eyes post, is flawed for general-purpose competitors. A new strategy is required.](https://fathom.video/share/as5MnWfH8BEhoZnsPXoXcLXFRr9TrzU2?tab=summary&timestamp=136.0)
  - [**Image Strategy Overhaul:** The current header images are ineffective. The new direction is stock photos without the full title text, using a shorter value-prop snippet and a lighter filter.](https://fathom.video/share/as5MnWfH8BEhoZnsPXoXcLXFRr9TrzU2?tab=summary&timestamp=870.0)
  - [**Process Improvements Approved:** Auto-publishing in Webflow is approved to accelerate the publishing workflow. Adding FAQs with schema to posts is also approved to boost SEO/AEO.](https://fathom.video/share/as5MnWfH8BEhoZnsPXoXcLXFRr9TrzU2?tab=summary&timestamp=646.0)

## Topics

### Holiday Content Pipeline

  - [**Problem:** The 1-post/day publishing cadence is at risk for the Dec 24–Jan 2 holiday break, as only 4 posts are ready.](https://fathom.video/share/as5MnWfH8BEhoZnsPXoXcLXFRr9TrzU2?tab=summary&timestamp=59.0)
  - [**Solution:** Everest will prioritize reviewing the 11 drafts in the "Ready to Review" queue to build a publishing buffer.](https://fathom.video/share/as5MnWfH8BEhoZnsPXoXcLXFRr9TrzU2?tab=summary&timestamp=529.0)
  - [**Context:** Mariana is revising 2 posts and will submit 5 new ideas by EOW, further expanding the review queue.](https://fathom.video/share/as5MnWfH8BEhoZnsPXoXcLXFRr9TrzU2?tab=summary&timestamp=546.0)

### Competitive Content Strategy

  - [**Problem:** Four competitive analysis drafts are blocked. Their structure, copied from a Zero Eyes post, is flawed because it focuses solely on gun detection—a narrow use case that doesn't fit general-purpose competitors.](https://fathom.video/share/as5MnWfH8BEhoZnsPXoXcLXFRr9TrzU2?tab=summary&timestamp=252.0)
  - [**Solution:** A new strategy is required.](https://fathom.video/share/as5MnWfH8BEhoZnsPXoXcLXFRr9TrzU2?tab=summary&timestamp=198.0)
      - [**GrowthX:** Create public-data profiles for each competitor to establish a baseline.](https://fathom.video/share/as5MnWfH8BEhoZnsPXoXcLXFRr9TrzU2?tab=summary&timestamp=388.0)
      - [**Ambient:** Share existing battle cards (by Monday) and provide field-based insights to enrich the profiles.](https://fathom.video/share/as5MnWfH8BEhoZnsPXoXcLXFRr9TrzU2?tab=summary&timestamp=220.0)
  - [**Outcome:** The combined data will form a "Competitive Positioning Guide" to ensure consistent, accurate messaging in future comparison posts.](https://fathom.video/share/as5MnWfH8BEhoZnsPXoXcLXFRr9TrzU2?tab=summary&timestamp=323.0)

### Blog Image Strategy

  - [**Problem:** Current header images are repetitive and ineffective. The full title text obscures the image, and the dark filter makes the background nearly invisible.](https://fathom.video/share/as5MnWfH8BEhoZnsPXoXcLXFRr9TrzU2?tab=summary&timestamp=870.0)
  - [**New Direction:**](https://fathom.video/share/as5MnWfH8BEhoZnsPXoXcLXFRr9TrzU2?tab=summary&timestamp=1457.0)
      - [**Format:** Stock photos (no AI-generated graphics).](https://fathom.video/share/as5MnWfH8BEhoZnsPXoXcLXFRr9TrzU2?tab=summary&timestamp=847.0)
      - [**Text:** A shorter, value-prop snippet instead of the full title.](https://fathom.video/share/as5MnWfH8BEhoZnsPXoXcLXFRr9TrzU2?tab=summary&timestamp=1457.0)
      - [**Aesthetics:** Lighter filter to make the background visible.](https://fathom.video/share/as5MnWfH8BEhoZnsPXoXcLXFRr9TrzU2?tab=summary&timestamp=1457.0)
  - [**Exploration:** Nicole is researching other companies' image strategies. Everest noted a preference for real/in-product shots over stylized designs, citing feedback from Shikhar (CEO) and the CTO.](https://fathom.video/share/as5MnWfH8BEhoZnsPXoXcLXFRr9TrzU2?tab=summary&timestamp=1142.0)

### Process & SEO Improvements

  - [**Auto-Publishing:** Approved. Mariana will gain direct publishing rights in Webflow, eliminating the manual ops ticket step to increase publishing velocity.](https://fathom.video/share/as5MnWfH8BEhoZnsPXoXcLXFRr9TrzU2?tab=summary&timestamp=646.0)
  - [**FAQs for SEO/AEO:** Approved. Adding 3–5 question FAQs with schema to post templates is expected to boost SEO and improve content visibility for LLMs.](https://fathom.video/share/as5MnWfH8BEhoZnsPXoXcLXFRr9TrzU2?tab=summary&timestamp=1560.0)
  - [**Source Library:** GrowthX is building a library of authoritative sources. Ambient requested prioritizing SIA (Security Industry Association) data.](https://fathom.video/share/as5MnWfH8BEhoZnsPXoXcLXFRr9TrzU2?tab=summary&timestamp=1638.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Everest:**](https://fathom.video/share/as5MnWfH8BEhoZnsPXoXcLXFRr9TrzU2?tab=summary&timestamp=529.0)
      - [Prioritize reviewing the 11 drafts in the "Ready to Review" queue.](https://fathom.video/share/as5MnWfH8BEhoZnsPXoXcLXFRr9TrzU2?tab=summary&timestamp=529.0)
      - [Share existing battle cards with GrowthX (by Monday).](https://fathom.video/share/as5MnWfH8BEhoZnsPXoXcLXFRr9TrzU2?tab=summary&timestamp=220.0)
  - [**GrowthX:**](https://fathom.video/share/as5MnWfH8BEhoZnsPXoXcLXFRr9TrzU2?tab=summary&timestamp=388.0)
      - [Create public-data profiles for the 4 competitors.](https://fathom.video/share/as5MnWfH8BEhoZnsPXoXcLXFRr9TrzU2?tab=summary&timestamp=388.0)
      - [Revise header image pipeline per new direction (stock photos, snippet text, lighter filter).](https://fathom.video/share/as5MnWfH8BEhoZnsPXoXcLXFRr9TrzU2?tab=summary&timestamp=1457.0)
      - [Implement auto-publishing in Webflow and add FAQ schema to the blog template.](https://fathom.video/share/as5MnWfH8BEhoZnsPXoXcLXFRr9TrzU2?tab=summary&timestamp=646.0)
      - [Send an async holiday update with the publishing schedule and emergency contact info.](https://fathom.video/share/as5MnWfH8BEhoZnsPXoXcLXFRr9TrzU2?tab=summary&timestamp=701.0)

action_items.description: Email Bailey battle cards/competitive materials; then GrowthX draft competitor profiles + positioning guide
Review blog articles 11–18; send feedback to Bailey
Set up Webflow auto-publishing; check/add FAQ schema; then GrowthX add 3–5 FAQ + schema to future posts
Email Everest async holiday update w/ publishing plan + POCs
Email designer re: header image revisions (stock, lighter, value-based text); share options w/ Everest + Nicole
Email Mariana re: SIA as preferred source
